\caption{Posterior-median IMF parameters for the Chabrier-like model with sampled mass cutoffs (\texttt{chabrier\_smooth\_bounds}), fit to each simulated stellar sample. Errors on $m_c$, $\Gamma$, $M_{\max}$ are 16th/84th posterior percentiles. $m_c$ is the lognormal characteristic mass, $\sigma$ is the lognormal width in dex, $\Gamma$ is the high-mass slope ($\mathrm{d}N/\mathrm{d}\log m \propto m^\Gamma$; Salpeter $=-1.35$), $M_{\max}$ is the upper-mass cutoff, SFE is the final star formation efficiency, $\dot{Q}_{\rm H}/M_*$ is the specific ionizing photon rate, and $L/M_*$ is the specific bolometric luminosity, both computed directly from the stellar catalog. For runs with multiple turbulent realizations, SFE is quoted as mean\,$\pm$\,std.}
\label{table:imf_params}
